name: Orunmila
description: Orunmila, also known as Ifa, is a prominent Orisha in Yoruba religion and its diaspora. He is revered as the Orisha of wisdom, knowledge, and divination. Orunmila witnessed the creation of the universe and possesses intimate knowledge of destiny, both individual and collective. He is the only Orisha who was present at the beginning of time and knows the fate of all things. Orunmila's primary function is to guide individuals towards their destiny through divination, using the sacred Opele chain or Ikin (palm nuts). He reveals the past, present, and future, offering solutions to problems and guiding followers to make informed decisions. Orunmila is not a warrior Orisha; his power lies in his profound understanding of the universe and his ability to interpret the divine will. He is a benevolent deity, dedicated to helping humanity achieve spiritual growth and fulfillment. Orunmila is considered the second in command to Olodumare, the supreme being, and acts as his intermediary in matters of divination and destiny. His teachings emphasize ethical conduct, respect for tradition, and the importance of seeking wisdom in navigating life's challenges. He is the patron of diviners (Babalawo) and divineresses (Iyanifa), who are trained in the complex system of Ifa divination. Orunmila's influence extends far beyond divination; he is a symbol of intellectual and spiritual enlightenment.
subtitle: Orisha of Wisdom and Divination

keyMyths.title: Orunmila Witnesses Creation
keyMyths.description: Orunmila was present at the creation of the universe, giving him unique insight into the workings of fate. He saw how Olodumare structured the cosmos and the destiny of all beings.
keyMyths.source: Oral traditions and Ifa literary corpus

keyMyths.title: Orunmila Teaches Ifa
keyMyths.description: Orunmila revealed the secrets of Ifa divination to humanity, allowing them to understand their destiny and navigate life's challenges. This knowledge is passed down through generations of diviners.
keyMyths.source: Oral traditions and Ifa literary corpus

keyMyths.title: Orunmila and the Lost Ase
keyMyths.description: A myth tells of Orunmila retrieving lost Ase (divine power) for humanity, emphasizing his role as a benefactor and protector of spiritual well-being. This showed his importance to humanity.
keyMyths.source: Oral traditions and Ifa literary corpus
